overall_verdict: Net positive. The workstream did the one thing the prior seven did not: it narrowed the gap to the actual goal (external use, n>1). lume now installs as a Claude plugin, resolves as a bare `lume`, bootstraps a fresh repo with `lume seed`, and ships a guiding skill + front-door README. The wrapper thesis held - the only product code written was the seed-bootstrap fix (ensure_lume_dir) the smoke test forced; everything else was packaging, docs, and config. Ceremony bought its cost: discovery caught the wrapper-not-rewrite shape early, and the operator gate caught two real structure calls (src layout, one guiding skill) before they ossified. A reject during closeout forced the packaging boundary fix (plugin content -> ./plugin/, so .lume/ never ships); done-when is now genuinely met bar one item: the end-to-end install is proven mechanically (subprocess over the bundled binary), not yet in a live Claude Code session.

stage_verdicts.saved: mapped plugin structure from docs and found find_lume_dir already decouples engine-location from state-location
stage_verdicts.net: high - reframed the whole workstream as a wrapper, not a rewrite

stage_verdicts.saved: 7 forks resolved with leans; F3 surfaced as the load-bearing call and sized first
stage_verdicts.net: positive - phasing held; no slice had to be re-cut

stage_verdicts.cost: git mv churn + one reject/redo cycle on flat-vs-src layout
stage_verdicts.saved: idiomatic src layout; history preserved; bin shim needed no logic change
stage_verdicts.net: positive - the reject was cheap and produced the better structure

stage_verdicts.saved: loop taught once, delegating specifics to the self-describing CLI so it won't drift
stage_verdicts.net: positive - minimal surface, no logic duplicated

stage_verdicts.cost: the seed-bootstrap fix + README + marketplace + settings + smoke test
stage_verdicts.saved: done-when made true: fresh-repo seed works; dogfooding wired; install path documented and smoke-tested
stage_verdicts.net: high - this is the slice that actually delivers installability

done_when.clause: Real package / entry point so `lume` resolves without a relative path
done_when.evidence: src/lume + bin/lume on PATH via plugin bin/; smoke test invokes bare bin/lume on a foreign dir
plugin.json + a guiding skill (F4: one guiding skill over per-verb commands) that drives the on-PATH lume; verbs reached via the self-describing CLI, not duplicated into glue

done_when.clause: Root README orienting a new operator
done_when.evidence: README.md: what/why, install, the loop, first command, verb table

done_when.clause: `lume seed` works as the new operator's first step
done_when.evidence: ensure_lume_dir creates .lume/ on a fresh repo; fresh-repo unit test + clean-repo smoke test both pass

done_when.clause: Existing tests still pass; live .lume/ unaffected
done_when.evidence: 313 green (was 311; +2 new); our own 0008 loop ran throughout the move via find_lume_dir

done_when.clause: A stranger could follow the README to their first iteration
done_when.evidence: smoke test drives seed->gates->new->open via the bundled binary; NOT yet exercised inside a real Claude Code session (needs install + trust prompt)

done_when.clause: Installing lume does not drag lume's own dev state into the adopter's cache
done_when.evidence: Reject-driven fix (F9): shipped content moved to ./plugin/, marketplace source './plugin'; .lume/, tests/, docs/ stay at repo root, outside the plugin. Confirmed plugin/ contains only bin/src/skills/README/plugin.json.

carry_forwards: Verify the plugin loads in a live Claude Code session (marketplace add + /plugin install on a fresh repo, or this repo's .claude/settings.json on next session+trust): confirm skills/lume/SKILL.md loads and bin/ is on PATH. This is the one done-when clause proven only mechanically.
Optional non-plugin install path (pyproject + console_script / zipapp) for users who want lume outside Claude Code - deferred in F1, not required by done-when.

The next workstream in the strategic chain is falsifiable value/cost measurement on real external use - the precondition the objective names before any autonomy. Packaging was its first gate; gather feedback from a real n>1 user next.
Operator convenience: add bin/lume to allowed Bash perms (the harness blocked the agent from doing so as self-modification).

sections.heading: What worked
sections.body: Discovery before code: confirming the self-describing verb catalog + find_lume_dir seam meant packaging was genuinely a wrapper. Only one piece of product code was needed (seed bootstrap), and the smoke test - not a human - is what forced it into the open. The operator gate earned its keep twice (src layout, single guiding skill).

sections.heading: What was harder than expected
sections.body: The done-when 'lume seed works on a fresh repo' was quietly false: every verb required a pre-existing .lume/. Discovery and planning both missed it; the clean-repo smoke test caught it. Lesson carried: a smoke test against the real entry point on a foreign dir is worth more than schema-level confidence.

sections.heading: Scope discipline
sections.body: Held the line against engine expansion: no review queue, no nesting, no measurement, no autonomy, no daemon/auto-upgrade. The one engine change (ensure_lume_dir) was onboarding-scoped and explicitly decided (F8) with the operator, not smuggled in.

sections.heading: The reject that mattered (packaging boundary)
sections.body: Closeout's done-when review claimed 'met' while quietly accepting that .lume/workstreams shipped with the plugin as inert data. The operator rejected that: adopting lume must not drag lume's dev history along. Research confirmed Claude Code has no exclude mechanism - the source tree copies wholesale - so the fix was structural: move shipped content into ./plugin/ and point the marketplace source there (F9, refining F3). Lesson: 'accept as inert' is a smell; an unwanted artifact that ships is a real defect, not a footnote. The operator gate caught what discovery, planning, and the agent's own closeout all waved through.
